symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden gross (macroscopic) haematuria with passage of clots

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible haematuria is the presenting flare of IgA nephropathy, though it is not specific in isolation.
Haematuria accompanies an obstructing/traumatising stone and cannot be excluded remotely.
Visible haematuria mandates exclusion of a bladder/upper-tract malignancy, especially with age.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe intermittent colicky flank pain (clot colic)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colicky loin-to-groin pain is the classic renal-colic pattern; the AI cannot distinguish stone from clot transit remotely.
Passage of blood clots down the ureter during a glomerular bleed produces colic that mimics a stone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Concurrent (synpharyngitic) upper-respiratory viral illness at haematuria onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Macroscopic haematuria concurrent with a mucosal viral infection (synpharyngitic timing) is characteristic of IgA nephropathy.
Post-streptococcal GN follows infection after a latency of weeks; concurrent timing argues against it.
A history of recurrent, self-limited, often painless visible haematuria is typical of relapsing IgA nephropathy flares.

symptoms.symptom_name: GP-detected persistent microscopic haematuria between visible episodes (patient-reported prior result)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Persistent inter-episode microscopic haematuria on a background of recurrent macroscopic flares is a classic IgA nephropathy pattern.
New hypertension in a young adult with glomerular haematuria suggests emerging chronic glomerular disease and warrants monitoring.

symptoms.symptom_name: No skin rash and no joint pain or swelling (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a purpuric rash and arthralgia argues against IgA vasculitis and other systemic vasculitides.
NSAID use can exacerbate glomerular bleeding and is nephrotoxic in impaired renal function; it modifies management (cease) rather than the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Still able to pass urine — no clot retention (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved voiding argues against current clot retention, but this can develop and is a mandatory safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, not dizzy or faint (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/rigors argues against an obstructed-infected kidney, and absence of faintness against significant blood-loss compromise — but neither can be measured remotely and both are safety-net triggers.

symptoms.symptom_name: Costovertebral-angle tenderness during clot transit (examination finding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild-to-moderate CVA tenderness during clot transit is a sealed exam finding the AI cannot elicit; it is non-specific between clot colic and a stone.
Mildly elevated blood pressure corroborates emerging glomerular disease; a sealed clinician measurement, no home cuff reported.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urinalysis: gross haematuria with mild-moderate proteinuria, negative for nitrites and leukocytes

investigation_details.expected_result: Gross haematuria with mild-to-moderate proteinuria; nitrite and leukocyte negative
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Glomerular bleeding with proteinuria unrecognised and a urinary tract infection not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haematuria with proteinuria and a bland infection-negative dipstick points to a glomerular (not infective) bleed.
Negative nitrites and leukocytes argue against a urinary tract infection as the cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Formal urine microscopy: dysmorphic red cells and red-cell casts (expected)

investigation_details.expected_result: Dysmorphic red blood cells and red-cell casts indicating a glomerular source
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Failure to confirm a glomerular (versus urological) origin of the bleeding.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Red-cell casts and dysmorphic red cells are diagnostic of glomerular bleeding, strongly supporting a glomerulonephritis such as IgA nephropathy.
Glomerular casts argue against a lower-tract or stone-related bleed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum urea, electrolytes and creatinine/eGFR, and full blood count (expected bloods)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Renal-function bloods and full blood count
investigation_details.expected_result: Assessment of renal function (possible mild impairment) and haemoglobin for significant blood loss
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Acute kidney injury and significant blood loss go unquantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Renal-function bloods characterise any acute-on-chronic impairment during the flare; a sealed investigation unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Renal biopsy: mesangial hypercellularity with IgA and C3 deposits on immunofluorescence (definitive, specialist-obtained)

investigation_details.expected_result: Mesangial hypercellularity with prominent IgA and C3 immune deposits
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The definitive diagnosis remains unconfirmed; biopsy is a specialist, non-acute investigation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mesangial IgA deposits on immunofluorescence are the defining biopsy finding of IgA nephropathy; obtained only by a specialist and never available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Visible haematuria concurrent with a mucosal viral illness, on a background of recurrent painless visible haematuria and persistent inter-episode microscopic haematuria, is the characteristic clinical signature of IgA nephropathy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person assessment with urine dipstick and formal urine microscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises the bleeding (glomerular red-cell casts vs urological) and screens for infection; cannot be obtained via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum urea, electrolytes, creatinine/eGFR and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies renal function during the flare and haemoglobin for blood loss.
Objective vital sign unobtainable via telehealth; relevant to emerging glomerular disease.
Excludes an obstructing stone or obstruction and assesses the kidneys given colicky flank pain with visible haematuria.
Quantifies proteinuria, a marker of glomerular disease activity and prognosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Nephrology referral for consideration of renal biopsy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive diagnosis of IgA nephropathy and long-term management is a specialist outpatient pathway, not an acute step.
Not indicated acutely in a young adult with a glomerular haematuria pattern; reserved for older patients or if a urological source is suspected after first-line work-up.
